archetype: 4 (Swiss & High-Contrast) adapted for Dark Mode Trading / Performance Pro

branding.name: LENSTEDREAL QUANTUM TERMINAL V10
branding.vibe: Extremely professional, Bloomberg Terminal equivalent, high-end fintech platform, pure data, no-nonsense.

layout.strategy: The 'Control Room' Grid (Mode B). Dense, functional, strict hierarchy.
layout.spacing: Tight padding (p-2 to p-4) for data density. Gap-2 or gap-4 for grid layouts to maximize screen real estate.
layout.grid_structure: grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-12. Main chart spans col-span-12 lg:col-span-8 or 9. Sidebars for intelligence/logs span col-span-12 lg:col-span-3 or 4.
layout.mobile_strategy: Mobile-first bottom-sheet navigation or stacked cards. The terminal grid must collapse gracefully to a single column on mobile, prioritizing the chart and active trades.

components.cards: Flat, sharp corners (rounded-sm or rounded-none), 1px solid border-zinc-800. No soft shadows. Use 'Grid Borders' look.
components.navigation: Dense top bar. Must include the LENSTEDREAL SYSTEMS logo, a fast-moving real-time ticker, and the Mobile App links ('Download on Play Store' button and 'App Store Coming Soon' badge) elegantly placed on the right side.
components.charts: Embed TradingView lightweight charts widget or Advanced Chart Widget. Must be forced into dark mode. Ensure it fills the container perfectly.
components.data_tables: Use JetBrains Mono for all tickers, prices, and percentages. Right-align numbers. Green for positive, Red for negative.
components.buttons: Sharp edges, minimal padding. Play Store button should be a high-contrast primary button with an icon. App Store badge should be a muted, inactive-looking element.
components.system_logs: A dedicated pane that looks like a raw developer terminal. Black background, monospace font, showing system events, order executions, and intelligence pings.
components.asset_intelligence: A panel showing AI-driven insights, technical metrics, and market scanners with progress bars or sparklines.

motion.animations: Minimal. Flash green/red backgrounds briefly when data updates. Very fast transitions (75ms - 150ms). No slow, bouncy animations.
motion.interactions: Hover states must be extremely clear for dense data rows (e.g., hover:bg-zinc-800/50).

image_urls.description: Subtle abstract wavy dark lines. Can be used at 5% opacity behind empty states or as a subtle watermark texture behind the Asset Intelligence panel.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install required dependencies: 'lucide-react' (or 'react-icons') for App Store/Play Store icons, and 'recharts' if building custom sparklines.
Add Google Fonts for 'IBM Plex Sans' and 'JetBrains Mono' to index.html.
Build a 'Control Room' grid layout using Tailwind grid-cols-12.
Force the app into dark mode universally (bg-zinc-950 text-zinc-50). Do not support light mode; it is a dark terminal.
Create the header: Logo on left, scrolling text marquee (ticker) in the center, and the mobile download badges on the right.
The 'Play Store' button should be active/clickable. The 'App Store' button should be a badge explicitly saying 'Coming Soon' (opacity-70, cursor-not-allowed).
Integrate the TradingView Advanced Chart Widget or Lightweight Chart in the main center panel (col-span-8 or 9).
Build the 'Asset Intelligence Panel' and 'Technical Metrics' on the right sidebar (col-span-3 or 4) with dense rows of data.
Build the 'System Activity Logs' panel at the bottom, using a strict monospace font, simulating a streaming command-line interface.
All numerical data (prices, percentages, volumes) MUST use the monospace font class (e.g., font-mono).
Ensure perfect alignment of all tabular data. Use sharp borders (border-zinc-800) to separate panes, akin to an IDE or Bloomberg Terminal.
